pi_items.metadata_value: Convert an image to pure black and white using GIMP's threshold tool
pi_items.verified_value: Task setup downloads grayscale image, opens in GIMP, instructions direct agent to Colors → Threshold, adjust slider, click OK. Verifier checks for: binary purity (≥85% pure BW or ≥90% extended), bimodal distribution (<15% mid-tones), original complexity (>20% mid-tones), meaningful transformation (>10% pixels changed).

pi_items.verified_value: 4 criteria evaluated: (1) binary purity ≥85% pure or ≥90% extended; (2) bimodal distribution with <15% mid-tones; (3) original had >20% mid-tones and >10 unique gray levels; (4) >10% pixel change. Pass requires ≥75% (3/4 criteria met).

privileged_info_summary: The threshold_binary task uses real, publicly accessible images from Unsplash (photo IDs 1518837695005-2083093ee35b and 1506905925346-21bda4d32df4) downloaded via HTTPS. Both images are 1000x667 RGB JPEGs with high tonal complexity suitable for threshold operations: primary image has 227 unique grayscale levels with 51.7% mid-tones; alternative has 247 unique levels with 77.3% mid-tones. Task runs on GIMP 2.10 with 90-second timeout and 7-step limit. The verifier checks for successful binary conversion by evaluating four criteria: binary purity (≥85% pure black/white or ≥90% near-extremes), bimodal distribution (<15% mid-tones), original complexity (>20% mid-tones), and meaningful transformation (>10% pixel change). A synthetic fallback image (800x600 with grayscale shapes) is generated via ImageMagick if downloads fail. Export is automated using xdotool/wmctrl GUI automation.
